content.full_text:
ROAR
Respect Our Animals' Rights!
915 L Street, Suite 1000, Sacramento, CA 95814

FPPC
JUN 13 9:16 AM '90

June 8, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sir/Madam:

This letter shall serve as my request for a formal opinion
on the following question:

Senator David Roberti founded and controlled an animal
rights political action committee, Respect Our Animals Rights (ROAR).
Effective January 1, 1989 the Senator resigned from the ROAR Board
(enclosure). Now the treasurer of a political action committee's
under the control of Senator Roberti is interested in sitting on the
ROAR Board. Would her position with Senator Roberti preclude her
from such activities and would this violate any FPPC rules?

Please address the letter to my attention at 915 L Street,
Suite 1000, Sacramento, CA 95814. Thank you.

Sincerely,

Karen L. Brainerd
Legislative Assistant

FOUNDER
David A. Roberti
State Senate President pro Tempore

BOARD OF DIRECTORS
Shirley Wechsler, Private Citizen
Allan Browne, Attorney
Gretchen Wyler, Actor
Duane Garrett, Attorney
David Nagler, Executive Vice President

ADVISORY BOARD
Tom Lantos, Congressman · California
Steve Kanaly, Actor
Earl Holliman, Actor
Gladys Sargent, Animal Activist
Dr. Donald Doyle, Physician
Dr. Michael Gignelli, Psychologist
Virginia Handley, Animal Activist
Loretta Swift, Actor
Sally Struthers, Actor

Margaret O'Brien, Actor
Tippi Hedron, Actor
Rhonda Fleming, Actor
Doug and Tina Trenner

Senator David Roberti
PRESIDENT PRO TEMPORE
CALIFORNIA STATE SENATE

December 31, 1988

Mr. David Nagler
Executive Director
R.O.A.R.
1010 - 11th Street, Suite 300
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear David,

Effective January 1, 1989, I am resigning from the Board
of Directors of R.O.A.R.

Best wishes.

Sincerely,

DAVID ROBERTI

DR/po

State Capitol, Room 205     Sacramento, CA 95814     Telephone: (916) 445-3300
